content: Comparative performance differences between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and general curious agents can be leveraged as an active selection signal to prune and prioritize rarely accessed episodic memories in state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active memory-augmented agents. Curiosity's intrinsic prediction error can double as a memory salience metric that automatically retains only high-uncertainty, high-learning-value memories and evicts stale, low-information ones.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
